markets.question: Frosinone Calcio vs. SSC Bari: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie B game between Frosinone Calcio and SSC Bari, scheduled for March 18 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Frosinone Calcio and SSC Bari combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaserieb.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Frosinone Calcio vs. SSC Bari: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie B game between Frosinone Calcio and SSC Bari, scheduled for March 18 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Frosinone Calcio and SSC Bari each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaserieb.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

markets.question: O/U 0.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Vitor Petrino and Steven Asplund at UFC Fight Night: Emmett vs. Vallejos, scheduled for March 14, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Vitor Petrino and Steven Asplund at UFC Fight Night: Emmett vs. Vallejos, scheduled for March 14, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 0.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 1.

“Under 0.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 1.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 1 for a 0.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond March 28, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC Fight Night: Vitor Petrino vs. Steven Asplund (Heavyweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Vitor Petrino" if Vitor Petrino is officially declared the winner of the fight against Steven Asplund at UFC Fight Night: Emmett vs. Vallejos, scheduled for March 14, 2026.

It will resolve to "Steven Asplund" if Steven Asplund is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond March 28, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

markets.question: Fight to Go the Distance?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fight between Andre Fili and Jose Miguel Delgado at UFC Fight Night: Emmett vs. Vallejos, scheduled for March 14, 2026, goes the full scheduled number of rounds and the result is determined by the judges' scorecards. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." 

Draws decided by the judges' scorecards after all scheduled rounds are completed will resolve "Yes." Technical decisions or technical draws declared before all rounds are completed will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond March 28, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.
